content: 2025 advancements in ICM curiosity-driven autonomous agents can reframe core reinforcement learning objective design: instead of aligning agents only with pre-defined external reward signals, we can enable RL systems to iteratively redefine their own task goals by exploring high-uncertainty environment gaps that match the agent's intrinsic curiosity, not just human-specified objectives. This turns traditional RL from a tool for solving fixed human problems into a self-directed problem-finding system for open-ended real-world domains like urban air mobility for swarm UAVs.
